name: The Nighthawk
tagline: Chicago-style comfort food in a historic Chinatown building — Italian beef sandwiches, Maxwell Street Polish, and late-night Kung Fu Ham.
mustOrder: Italian beef sandwich — Chicago-style, juicy and proper
Chicago-style hot dog — no ketchup, ever
Kung Fu Ham — deep-fried pork belly with Thai chili garlic
parking: Street parking on S King St; paid lots on Smith/Maunakea
talkStory: A Midwest transplant concept that somehow feels perfectly at home in Chinatown. Exposed brick walls, late-night hours, and a menu of Chicago comfort food you can't get anywhere else on the island. The Italian beef sandwich is authentic. The Kung Fu Ham (deep-fried pork belly with Thai chili garlic) is a house original that bridges Chicago and Honolulu. Downtown workers for lunch, Chinatown bar crowd for late night. Open until midnight.
